mission: Stop relying on expensive corporate subscriptions just to access powerful, reliable AI. Our lightweight engine runs directly on your own hardware, giving your local models the enterprise-grade reasoning and accuracy of giant cloud servers for a fraction of the cost.

source_policy.scope: First mining pass over current frontier cards and advanced local/open-weight model cards.
source_policy.note: This is a seed corpus for benchmark design, not an exhaustive model registry.

signals: coding plus research plus documents and spreadsheets
checks work and persists until done
benchmark_implications: Measure task completion across multi-tool trajectories, not only final text quality.
Include document, spreadsheet, browser, code, and shell task lanes.
Add persistence-with-boundaries metrics: continues when useful, stops or asks when scope changes.
Report all major scores by effort budget instead of single-score leaderboards.
Add intent-boundary metrics for over-persistent agents.
Add trajectory resampling/simulator lane for tool-call realism.
Add self-improvement tasks: research debugging, kernel optimization, training-loop optimization, post-training recipe design, and MLE-style experiments.

signals: task failures must reflect model limits, not benchmark flaws
benchmark_implications: Every custom benchmark task needs a quality-audit label.
Track overly strict tests, underspecified prompts, low-coverage tests, and misleading prompts.
Report pass rates with broken-task-adjusted pass rates.
Create a pre-run audit harness that inspects prompt/test/reference alignment before scoring models.
Compare harnesses on terminal-task reliability, not only model response quality.
Add team/subagent coordination metrics.
Track context compaction quality: retained decisions, lost constraints, and stale-state errors.
Expose intelligence-speed-cost curves for each local and frontier backend.
Add MCP workflow benchmarks as first-class metrics.
Add long-context degradation tests at 32k, 128k, and 1M-equivalent retrieval setups.
Add multimodal/document-grounded tasks when local backends support them.

signals: coding, agentic tasks, and knowledge work
DeepSWE, Terminal Bench, and SWE Bench Pro reporting
data filtering, deduplication, quality scoring, and domain-focused selection
RL over hundreds of thousands of multi-step technical tasks
80 TPS serving claim and token-efficiency framing
Grok Build, Cursor, Office, and API availability
benchmark_implications: Add token-efficiency metrics beside accuracy and latency.
Benchmark office-work artifacts: spreadsheets, slides, diagrams, and documents.
Track one-prompt app-building quality with artifact inspection rather than screenshots alone.
Track data-quality/flywheel claims as measurable curation and deduplication variables.
Add free/account-backed harness lanes where available instead of treating paid API absence as a blocker.

signals: agentic coding, biology, and cybersecurity focus
benchmark_implications: Score intelligence per token, dollar, and wall-clock time.
Separate flagship, balanced-cost, and fast-cost lanes for local model routing.
Add cache-breakpoint/reuse economics to long-context harness runs.
Track defender-oriented vulnerability-finding tasks separately from exploit-execution tasks.

signals: Fable 5 is a Mythos-class model made safe for general use
longer and more complex tasks increase Fable lead
Fable includes safeguards and fallback behavior
Mythos 5 is trusted-access with higher-risk domain capability
agentic coding, prototyping, research, legal, finance, and spreadsheet customer evidence
benchmark_implications: Add long-horizon task duration and context-retention metrics.
Measure fallback/refusal routing as first-class integration behavior.
Track subagent delegation, self-checking, and work validation.
Separate public-safeguarded and trusted-access capability profiles in harness comparisons.
Measure thinking vs non-thinking modes separately.
Record quantization, context length, sampling parameters, and runtime backend.
Use as a candidate baseline for the 14B local model lane.
Use as a candidate baseline for the 32B local model lane.
Measure tool-call correctness, parser compatibility, and MCP tool-use recovery.
Benchmark long-context with and without YaRN because long-context scaling can affect short-context performance.

signals: integration with Claude Code, Qwen Code, Qoder, Kilo, Trae, and Cline
benchmark_implications: Use as the model-card reference for recovery-from-failure task lanes.
Measure active-parameter efficiency separately from total-parameter size.
Add CLI/IDE harness portability tests.
Treat sampling profile as a benchmark variable, not a hidden constant.
Separate precise-coding sampling from general reasoning sampling.
Track repetition and language-mixing failure modes.

signals: single RTX 4090 or Mac 32GB local deployment target
benchmark_implications: Add codebase-exploration tasks with multi-file edit scoring.
Track local hardware envelope: VRAM/RAM, tokens/sec, energy/cost proxy, and pass rate.
Use OpenHands-style task shape as a comparative harness lane.

local_open_weight_sources.size: 15B params on Hugging Face model metadata
local_open_weight_sources.context: 32,768 max generation length used in reported benchmark setup
signals: reasoning distillation into smaller dense models
benchmark_implications: Use as a candidate reasoning baseline for the 14B lane.
Run repeated samples and report variance, not just one-shot pass rate.
Track reasoning-init failures where thinking content is skipped.

local_open_weight_sources.context: 32,768 max generation length used in reported benchmark setup
signals: strong public benchmark claims across math, code, and reasoning
benchmark_implications: Use as a candidate reasoning baseline for the 32B lane.
Compare against Qwen3-32B on agent recovery, code execution, and benchmark robustness.
Track exact generation parameters and multi-sample aggregation.
Add a multimodal local-model lane when the harness can provide images/doc screenshots.
Track license constraints independently from raw model capability.
Use as local multimodal comparison rather than primary coding baseline.
